response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive experience in software development, including low-latency systems, hardware-related projects, and system architecture, but his background is heavily skewed toward general-purpose software engineering rather than deep specialization in AI accelerators, SoC/IP modeling, or hardware-software co-design for AI workloads. His education does not meet the required advanced degree thresholds, and his professional focus lacks direct experience with deep-learning optimization or GPU architectures.
strengths: Strong system architecture and hardware-software co-design experience (e.g., robotic arms, real-time systems).
Proven ability to optimize performance in high-load environments (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests per second).
Experience with low-level programming (C, C++, Dlang) and hardware interactions, which is relevant to AI accelerator work.
concerns: Limited direct experience with AI accelerators, deep-learning workload optimization, or GPU architectures as required for the role.
Lacks the preferred advanced degree (PhD/Master's) in a relevant engineering or CS field, with only a Bachelor's degree.
No clear evidence of work on SoC/IP modeling, performance bottleneck analysis for deep-learning workloads, or system-on-chip design feedback.
